label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I have a 2x2x4 box and seven 3x1x1 waffles. I need to figure out how many waffles I should eat so that the remaining ones can fit into the box without breaking any. Hmm. Let me compute its volume: 2 * 2 * 4 = 16 cubic units. Each waffle is 3x1x1, so each has a volume of 3 cubic units. With seven waffles, the total volume is 7 * 3 = 21 cubic units. The box's volume is only 16, so even if I could perfectly pack them without any wasted space, I could only fit 16 / 3 ≈ 5.33 waffles. But in the other dimensions, the box is only 2 units. So if the waffle is 3 units in one dimension, it can't fit if we try to place it along the 2-unit side. Therefore, the waffle must be oriented so that its 3-unit length is along the 4-unit side of the box. So each waffle must be placed such that their 3-unit dimension is along the 4-unit side of the box. Now, let's consider how many can fit in the box. The box is 2x2x4.
